---
title: "2.3 if Statements"
subtitle: "Selection and Iteration"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit2-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## if Statements

### Turn Boolean conditions into branching code

An <span class="term">if statement</span> changes the default sequential flow of execution.

::: {.tight-list}
- when the condition is `true`, one branch runs
- when the condition is `false`, the program skips that branch or takes another one
:::

![Comparing block-based and Java ifs](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-3-if-statements/BlocksIfComparison.png){fig-align="center" width="86%"}

## One-Way Selection

### Run code only when the condition is true

![One-way conditional flow](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-3-if-statements/Condition.png){fig-align="center" width="30%"}

```java
if (isRaining)
{
    System.out.println("Take an umbrella!");
}
System.out.println("Drive carefully");
```

If the condition is false, the body is skipped and execution continues after the `if`.

## Conditions Inside if Statements

### Most branches use relational operators

```java
if (number > 0)
{
    System.out.println(number + " is positive!");
}
if (number == 0)
{
    System.out.println(number + " is zero!");
}
```

Students need to see that `if` statements do not create new logic on their own.  
They <span class="mark">consume Boolean expressions</span>.

## Two-Way Selection

### `if ... else` handles both outcomes

::: {.two-col}
::: {}
![if/else flowchart](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-3-if-statements/Condition-two.png){width="100%"}
:::
::: {.soft-box}
Use `if/else` when exactly one of two branches must run:

- one branch for `true`
- one branch for `false`
- one test case for each branch
:::
:::

```java
if (age >= 16)
{
    System.out.println("You can get a license.");
}
else
{
    System.out.println("You need to wait.");
}
```

## Common Errors with if Statements

### Most mistakes are structural, not conceptual

::: {.tight-list}
- forgetting curly braces for the intended block
- writing `if (test);` with a stray semicolon
- using `=` instead of `==` in the condition
- assuming `else` matches indentation instead of the actual closest `if`
:::

The AP-style habit is simple: <span class="mark">always use braces</span>.

## Classroom Task

### Magic 8 Ball

![Magic 8 Ball challenge](../../assets/2-selection-and-iteration/2-3-if-statements/Magic_eight_ball.png){fig-align="center" width="24%"}

Retained task focus:

::: {.tight-list}
- use random numbers to choose a branch
- write multiple `if` checks or `if/else` logic
- connect Boolean tests to visible program behavior
- <span class="term">2.3.5 Coding Challenge: Magic 8 Ball</span>
:::

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- explain that `if` statements change control flow
- distinguish one-way and two-way selection
- use relational operators correctly inside conditions
- explain why both branches of `if/else` need testing
- avoid `=`, stray semicolons, and missing braces in conditional code
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
